disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The joint pain has migrated across several joints rather than staying fixed in one — right knee, then left ankle, then both wrists
trigger_question_examples: Has the pain stayed in the same joints, or has it moved around?
Which joints are affected, and did they come on at the same time or one after another?
Is it in one joint or several?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It keeps moving — it started in my right knee, then my left ankle, and now it's both wrists. It doesn't stay in one place.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There was a widespread blotchy pink (maculopapular) rash a few days before the joint pain that has since faded
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any rash or skin changes recently?
Did you notice anything on your skin before the joints started hurting?
Any spots, blotches, or rash anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, actually — a few days ago I had a blotchy pink rash, but it's faded now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Preceding several days of marked fatigue and feeling generally unwell before the joints became painful
trigger_question_examples: How have you been feeling in yourself over the past week?
Did you feel unwell or run down before the joint pain started?
Any tiredness, fevers, or feeling generally sick beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been completely wiped out for about five days now, even before the joints started.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No single joint has become bright red, intensely hot, or dramatically more swollen than the others, and there has been no rigor or shaking chills or high fever
trigger_question_examples: Has any one joint become bright red, very hot to touch, or much more swollen than the others?
Have you had any shaking chills, rigors, or a high fever?
Is there one joint that is dramatically worse and hot rather than the pain moving around?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — none of them have gone bright red or really hot, and I haven't had shaking chills. It's more that the ache keeps moving around.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: They all just ache — I hadn't really checked if one was hotter than the others.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and is currently unable to open medication bottles or prepare food because of wrist pain
trigger_question_examples: Are you managing okay at home on your own tonight?
Is anyone with you, and can you do the basics like eating and taking medication?
How are you coping with everyday tasks with your wrists like this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own, and honestly I can't even open a jar or a bottle or make myself food tonight.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can you just give me something strong for the pain so I can sleep tonight? The paracetamol did nothing.
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledge the distress and sleep loss without prescribing a potent anti-inflammatory de novo over telehealth before serious mimics are excluded. A good response explains that severe migrating joint pain needs a same-day hands-on assessment and blood tests to rule out a joint infection before strong medication is started, and arranges that assessment rather than simply issuing a script.
